summary.intent: Fix the dcg-guard hook silently failing open when 'dcg' is not on the gateway process PATH, particularly under systemd where $HOME/.local/bin is stripped from the environment.
summary.outcome: Three-commit fix landed on PR #635: handler script augments PATH before resolving dcg, emits loud stderr warning when dcg is missing, startup check logs guard state at boot, HOOK.md install instructions corrected, stale handler auto-refresh on next restart, async subprocess for version probe, and HOME fallback parity between Rust and shell. Seven unit tests added, all passing.

repo: Hooks always run in the gateway process (not the sandbox), inheriting the gateway env — confirmed at crates/plugins/src/shell_hook.rs:101-117. The sandbox PATH is irrelevant to hook resolution.

seed_dcg_guard_hook() originally gated all seeding on HOOK.md existence, meaning existing installs with stale pre-fix handlers were never refreshed — a silent regression for all users already affected by #626.
The 'which' crate is a workspace dependency but hook_eligibility.rs:63-70 uses a std::process::Command('which') idiom instead; both patterns exist in the repo.
Local E2E Validation CI check requires running ./scripts/local-validate.sh <PR_NUMBER> locally and posting a status — it does not run automatically in GitHub Actions.
The dcg install instruction 'cargo install dcg' seeded in HOOK.md never worked; upstream only supports 'uv tool install destructive-command-guard' and 'pipx install destructive-command-guard'.
sibling seed_* helpers in server.rs use synchronous file I/O; only the subprocess version probe needed to be async.

code.finding: DCG_GUARD_HANDLER_SH: original fail-open branch was completely silent (cat >/dev/null; exit 0) with no logging, making it impossible to detect an inert guard in gateway logs.
seed_dcg_guard_hook(): the early-return on create_dir_all failure suppressed the startup status log entirely — operators saw nothing about guard state on boot.
DCG_GUARD_HOOK_MD: contained 'cargo install dcg' which has never worked per upstream README.
ShellHookHandler spawns sh -c inheriting the gateway process environment — PATH narrowness is a gateway concern, not a sandbox concern.

workflow: Greptile automated code review caught two real bugs in follow-up commits: (1) blocking std::process::Command inside async context, (2) HOME-unset fallback mismatch between Rust probe and shell handler.
Fingerprint-based file refresh (checking for a known marker string rather than file existence) is a clean pattern for auto-remediating stale seeded files without a schema version field.
When a shell handler and Rust code both compute the same directory list, introduce a shared constant (DCG_GUARD_HOME_FALLBACK) and reference it in both places to prevent future drift.
Killing a long-running local validator (clippy full workspace) and restarting after fixing newly discovered issues is preferable to letting it complete on stale code.
Replying directly to inline review comments with the fixing commit SHA is a good practice to close the feedback loop without waiting for re-review.

friction: Local E2E Validation CI gate requires a manual local script invocation (./scripts/local-validate.sh <PR>) — not discoverable from the PR status check alone; had to inspect the failing run log to find this.
local-validate.sh runs a full workspace clippy which takes long enough that it had to be killed and restarted twice after new fixes were committed.
Greptile's review comments were spread across both inline PR comments and a top-level summary comment, requiring separate API calls (pulls/635/comments vs issues/635/comments) to enumerate them all.
The 'which' crate availability had to be verified by grepping Cargo.toml before use — it was present in the workspace but not in the gateway crate's own Cargo.toml.
open_items: Manual QA on a real systemd deployment still pending: delete ~/.moltis/hooks/dcg-guard/, restart Moltis, confirm startup log line appears.
Manual QA with dcg installed: confirm rm -rf through exec tool is blocked.
Manual QA with dcg uninstalled: confirm 'NOT scanned' warning appears per-invocation in gateway logs.
Full 'just lint' run on Linux CI — local macOS run timed out on unrelated CUDA feature build; gateway crate was linted directly with --features local-llm-metal as a workaround.
CI checks for commit 1009f80d and the HOME-fallback commit were still queued/running at end of session — no confirmation all checks passed.
Local E2E Validation script needs to be re-run against the final HEAD commit after killing the in-progress run.
